title: 10 best herbal tinctures for bad breath
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for bad breath are concentrated liquid extracts made from herbs that have natural antibacterial and antimicrobial properties, which can help eliminate the cause of bad breath. These tinctures can be used as a natural and effective alternative to traditional mouthwashes. Examples of herbal tinctures that help with bad breath include tea tree, sage, peppermint, and clove. These herbs work by killing the bacteria that cause bad breath, leaving the mouth feeling fresh and clean. By using herbal tinctures, people can improve their breath and overall oral health, increasing their confidence in social situations and improving their quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tincture helps with bad breath because it contains antibacterial and antiseptic properties that can kill the bacteria causing bad breath. The main active ingredient in peppermint, menthol, has natural antibacterial properties that can eliminate the germs responsible for causing foul breath. Additionally, peppermint has a strong, refreshing smell that can help to mask any lingering bad odors in the mouth. Furthermore, peppermint tincture can stimulate the production of saliva, which helps to wash away food particles and bacteria that can contribute to bad breath. Overall, peppermint tincture is a natural and effective way to combat bad breath and promote fresh, clean breath.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Peppermint tincture inhibits the growth of microorganisms that cause bad breath, thanks to its active constituents like menthol, menthone, and limonene.

Anti-inflammatory: It reduces inflammation in the mouth and throat, providing relief from irritation and pain, which can contribute to bad breath, due to compounds such as rosmarinic acid and flavonoids.

Antioxidant: Peppermint tincture neutralizes free radicals, protecting oral tissues from damage and supporting overall oral health, attributed to its high levels of vitamin C and other antioxidant compounds.

Carminative: It helps prevent the formation of gas in the gastrointestinal tract, reducing digestive issues that can cause bad breath, owing to its volatile oils, including menthol and methyl salicylate.

Flavoring and deodorizing: Peppermint tincture provides a refreshing taste and deodorizing effect, eliminating bad breath caused by oral bacteria and food particles, due to its potent aromatic compounds, primarily menthol and limonene.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in menthol and menthone, the leaves are commonly used to make medicinal tinctures for bad breath due to their potent antibacterial and freshening properties.
Flowers: Delicate and aromatic, peppermint flowers contain essential oils that help neutralize odor-causing bacteria, making them an excellent addition to medicinal tinctures for bad breath.
Seeds: Packed with antioxidants and essential oils, peppermint seeds can be used in tinctures to provide long-lasting freshness and combat halitosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves by cutting off stems from mature plants with clean scissors or pruning shears.
Rinse the harvested peppermint leaves gently with cool water to remove any dirt or debris, then pat dry.
Weigh and chop 200g of dried peppermint leaves into small pieces, ensuring evenness for efficient extraction.
Combine the chopped peppermint leaves with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a glass jar, leaving about 2 inches at the top for liquid expansion during infusion.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place and allow it to infuse for 6 weeks, shaking daily, before straining and filtering the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Spearmint tincture helps with bad breath because it contains antimicrobial and antifungal properties that fight the bacteria and fungi causing bad breath. Its active compounds, such as carvone and limonene, have been shown to inhibit the growth of oral microorganisms that produce volatile sulfur compounds (VSCs), which are responsible for bad breath. Moreover, spearmint has a refreshing and strong aroma that can mask unpleasant odors and leave a long-lasting fresh sensation in the mouth. Regular use of spearmint tincture can help maintain oral hygiene, reduce plaque, and promote a healthy and fresh breath.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Spearmint tincture's antimicrobial properties help reduce the growth of bacteria responsible for bad breath, as it contains active compounds such as carvone and limonene.
Antioxidant: Its antioxidant properties help protect oral tissues from damage and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to bad breath, due to the presence of rosmarinic acid and flavonoids.

Anti-inflammatory: Spearmint tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help alleviate irritation and swelling in the mouth and gums, which can lead to bad breath, thanks to the action of rosmarinic acid and other polyphenols.

Carminative: The carminative properties of spearmint tincture aid in reducing digestive issues such as gas and bloating, which can cause bad breath, as it contains volatile oils like carvone and limonene.
Fresh breath: Spearmint tincture provides a refreshing taste and aroma that helps mask bad breath, while its active compounds continue to work against the underlying causes of halitosis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Spearmint leaves are rich in menthol, which helps to freshen breath and have antimicrobial properties that can kill the bacteria causing bad breath.
Flowers: Spearmint flowers contain essential oils that have a refreshing aroma, making them useful in tinctures to combat bad breath.
Seeds: Spearmint seeds are a concentrated source of the plant's active compounds, and they can be used to make tinctures that help to neutralize bad breath odors.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh spearmint leaves ensuring they are free of pests and diseases for optimal quality.
Chop the harvested spearmint leaves into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine one part chopped spearmint with three parts 95% ethanol in a glass jar to create the maceration mixture.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for four to six weeks, shaking the jar daily, allowing for gentle extraction of active compounds.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another container and discard the solids before transferring the tincture to dark glass bottles for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tincture helps with bad breath because it has antibacterial and antimicrobial properties that can kill the bacteria in the mouth responsible for bad breath. The compound citral found in lemon balm inhibits the growth of these bacteria, reducing the production of volatile sulfur compounds that cause bad breath. Additionally, lemon balm has a refreshing and calming effect on the digestive system, which can help prevent bad breath caused by indigestion or stomach issues. Its pleasant lemon scent also leaves a fresh and pleasant smell in the mouth.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Lemon balm tincture inhibits the growth of bacteria and microorganisms responsible for bad breath, such as Streptococcus mutans and Candida albicans, due to its active constituents, primarily eugenol, citral, and geraniol.

Carminative: Lemon balm tincture helps reduce intestinal gas and bloating, which can contribute to bad breath, by promoting the expulsion of gas from the stomach and intestines, thanks to its active compound citral.

Anxiolytic: Lemon balm tincture has calming and relaxing effects, which can help alleviate stress-induced bad breath by reducing tension and promoting overall oral health, owing to rosmarinic acid and eugenol.

Antioxidant: Lemon balm tincture contains potent antioxidants that protect oral tissues from oxidative damage and inflammation, which can lead to periodontal disease and halitosis, due to its rich polyphenol content.

Anti-inflammatory: Lemon balm tincture reduces inflammation and swelling in the oral cavity, which can contribute to bad breath, by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, with rosmarinic acid and eugenol being the primary active compounds responsible for this effect.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm are commonly used in making medicinal tinctures for bad breath due to their high concentration of volatile oils, which have antibacterial and antifungal properties that can help eliminate the bacteria and fungi responsible for bad breath.

Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and fight off infections in the mouth and throat, thereby reducing bad breath.

Stems: While not as potent as the leaves or flowers, the stems of lemon balm still contain some of the beneficial compounds found in the plant, making them useful for making medicinal tinctures for bad breath.

Seeds: The seeds of lemon balm contain essential oils and other compounds that can help stimulate the production of saliva, which can help wash away bacteria and food particles that contribute to bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves and stems at peak potency for optimal flavor and aroma.
Dry the harvested plant material using air circulation or desiccants for 24-48 hours.
Chop dried melissa officinalis into small pieces and combine with a carrier oil such as glycerin for a ratio of 1:4.
Steep the mixture in a clean glass jar at room temperature for 2-4 weeks shaking daily for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into dark glass bottles and store in cool dark places.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tincture helps with bad breath because it contains antibacterial and astringent properties that help eliminate the bacteria in the mouth responsible for causing bad breath. The compounds in sage, such as salvianolic acid and rosmarinic acid, inhibit the growth of odor-causing microorganisms, reducing the production of volatile sulfur compounds. Additionally, sage's astringent properties help tighten and constrict oral tissues, reducing the likelihood of food particles becoming trapped and decomposing, which can lead to bad breath. By using sage tincture as a mouthwash or gargle, you can effectively freshen your breath and maintain oral hygiene.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Sage tincture inhibits the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms in the mouth that cause bad breath, due to the presence of active compounds like camphor, eucalyptol, and thujone.
Astringent: Sage tincture tightens and contracts oral tissues, reducing the production of excess saliva that can lead to bad breath, due to its tannin content.

Anti-inflammatory: Sage tincture reduces inflammation in the gums and mouth, which can help alleviate bad breath caused by gum disease or other inflammatory conditions, due to the presence of rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid.
Antioxidant: Sage tincture neutralizes free radicals and protects oral tissues from damage, reducing the risk of bad breath caused by oxidative stress, due to its high polyphenol content.
Anesthetic: Sage tincture numbs and soothes oral tissues, providing relief from pain or discomfort that can contribute to bad breath, due to the presence of camphor and other volatile oils.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the sage plant contain powerful oils that can help to eliminate the bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath.
Leaves: The leaves of the sage plant have antimicrobial and astringent properties that can help to freshen breath and reduce inflammation in the mouth.
Flowers: The flowers of the sage plant contain high levels of antioxidants that can help to neutralize the volatile sulfur compounds that cause bad breath.
Seeds: The seeds of the sage plant can be used to make a tincture that can help to stimulate the production of saliva, which can help to wash away bacteria and food particles that cause bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried salvia officinalis leaves from your garden or purchase them from a reputable source.
Cleanly sort through the leaves removing any stems or debris and grind into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 100g of ground sage with 500ml of high-proof vodka or brandy in a clean glass jar ensuring all material is submerged in liquid.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
After the preparation time filter the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids and label the finished product.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tincture helps with bad breath because it contains powerful antiseptic and antibacterial properties that can kill the bacteria responsible for bad breath. The herb's volatile oils, such as thymol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of oral bacteria, reducing the production of volatile sulfur compounds that cause unpleasant odors. Additionally, thyme has anti-inflammatory effects that can help soothe irritated gums and promote overall oral health. By using thyme tincture as a mouthwash or gargle, you can effectively eliminate bad breath and maintain a fresh and clean mouth.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial: Thyme tincture's antibacterial properties help eliminate the bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath, thanks to its active constituents like thymol and carvacrol.
Antifungal: Its antifungal properties inhibit the growth of fungi in the mouth, reducing the risk of oral thrush and bad breath, due to the presence of thymol and other volatile oils.

Antioxidant: Thyme tincture's high antioxidant content helps protect the mouth from oxidative stress and damage, promoting overall oral health and fresh breath, due to its rich concentration of phenolic compounds.

Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of thyme tincture help reduce inflammation in the gums and mouth, alleviating conditions that can cause bad breath, thanks to the presence of flavonoids like apigenin and luteolin.

Spasmolytic: Thyme tincture's spasmolytic properties help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing the likelihood of halitosis caused by indigestion or acid reflux, due to the active constituent thymol.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are rich in essential oils, including thymol, which has antibacterial properties that can help eliminate the bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain similar essential oils as the leaves, and can be used interchangeably to make medicinal tinctures for bad breath.
Stems: Thyme stems contain trace amounts of essential oils, and can be included in tinctures to add extra plant material and increase the potency of the final product.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thyme leaves from the garden or store-dried thyme in small quantities to ensure optimal potency.
Chop the harvested thyme into smaller pieces and combine with high-proof vodka at a ratio of one part herb to three parts solvent.
Store the mixture in an amber glass jar away from direct sunlight for a minimum of four weeks, shaking the bottle daily.
After the maceration period, strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Transfer the clear liquid to dark glass bottles, label, and store at room temperature for up to two years, monitoring the potency regularly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oregano tincture helps with bad breath because it contains compounds like carvacrol and thymol, which have antimicrobial properties. These compounds can help reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath. Additionally, oregano has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe inflamed gums, which can also contribute to bad breath. Furthermore, oregano tincture can help stimulate the production of saliva, which helps to wash away food particles and bacteria that can cause bad breath. Overall, oregano tincture can be an effective natural remedy for bad breath due to its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and saliva-stimulating properties.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial: Oregano tincture's antibacterial properties help reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath, due to the presence of carvacrol and thymol.
Antifungal: Its antifungal properties help inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, a type of fungus that can cause bad breath, thanks to the active constituents thymol and carvacrol.
Anti-inflammatory: Oregano tincture's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the gums and mouth, which can contribute to bad breath, due to the presence of beta-caryophyllene.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of oregano tincture help protect the mouth and gums from damage caused by free radicals, which can lead to bad breath, thanks to the high concentration of rosmarinic acid.
Immune-boosting: Oregano tincture's immune-boosting properties can help the body fight off infections that can cause bad breath, due to the presence of a variety of phenolic acids and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Often used in medicinal tinctures due to their high concentration of essential oils, which can help neutralize bad breath.
Flowers: Contain potent antimicrobial compounds that can eliminate the bacteria causing bad breath.
Seeds: Rich in thymol and carvacrol, which have antiseptic properties that can freshen breath and improve oral health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality oregano vulgare leaves by hand or mechanically to ensure freshness and potency.
Clean the harvested leaves thoroughly with cold running water to remove dirt and debris.
Dehydrate the cleaned oregano leaves in a low-temperature oven at 105°f for 30 minutes to preserve their essential oils.
Combine one part dried oregano herb with two parts high-proof vodka or ethanol in a clean glass jar, securing lid tightly.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for four weeks, shaking occasionally, before straining and filtering to obtain clear tincture liquid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary tincture helps with bad breath because it contains antibacterial and antimicrobial properties that fight against the bacteria and microorganisms causing bad breath. Its strong aroma and anti-inflammatory properties also help to eliminate the odor caused by volatile sulfur compounds (VSCs) in the mouth. Furthermore, rosemary tincture stimulates the production of saliva, which helps to wash away food particles and bacteria that can lead to bad breath. Regular use of rosemary tincture can significantly improve oral hygiene and freshen breath naturally.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Rosemary tincture inhibits the growth of bacteria and microorganisms responsible for bad breath, providing a clean and fresh mouth. This property is due to the presence of active compounds such as camphor, cineole, and eucalyptol.

Anti-inflammatory: By reducing inflammation in the oral cavity, rosemary tincture helps alleviate irritation and redness, which can contribute to bad breath. This is attributed to the high concentration of rosmarinic acid and other phenolic compounds.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content of rosemary tincture helps protect oral tissues from damage caused by free radicals, contributing to overall oral health and preventing bad breath. This is due to the presence of powerful antioxidants like carnosol, carnosic acid, and rosmarinic acid.
Astringent: Rosemary tincture helps tighten and tone gum tissues, reducing the likelihood of gum inflammation and halitosis. This property is attributed to the tannins present in rosemary.

Deodorizing: The deodorizing properties of rosemary tincture neutralize unpleasant odors, leaving the mouth fresh and clean. This is due to the presence of volatile oils like camphor, cineole, and eucalyptol, which provide a refreshing aroma.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rosemary leaves are rich in chlorophyll and have antibacterial properties, making them effective at reducing bad breath caused by oral bacteria.
Flowers: Rosemary flowers contain volatile oils that can help eliminate the bacteria causing bad breath and provide a pleasant aroma.
Stems: Rosemary stems contain essential oils and tannins that have astringent properties, helping to reduce inflammation and fight bad breath.
Seeds: Rosemary seeds are a potent source of antioxidants and have antimicrobial properties, making them useful in treating bad breath caused by infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosemary leaves from healthy plants and rinse them gently with filtered water.
Combine 100g of dried rosemary leaves with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 6 weeks in a cool dark place, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container and discard solids.
Store the final tincture in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cinnamon tincture helps with bad breath because it contains powerful antibacterial and antifungal properties that can kill the bacteria in the mouth responsible for causing bad breath. The main active component in cinnamon, called cinnamaldehyde, has been shown to inhibit the growth of oral bacteria, reducing the production of volatile sulfur compounds that cause bad breath. Additionally, cinnamon has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the gums and mouth, further preventing bad breath. Its pleasant taste and aroma also make it a popular natural breath freshener. Regular use of cinnamon tincture can help maintain a healthy oral microbiome and promote fresh breath.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Cinnamon tincture's antimicrobial properties help eliminate the bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath, thanks to its active constituents such as cinnamaldehyde and eugenol.

Anti-inflammatory: Its anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation in the gums and mouth, providing relief from conditions that can worsen bad breath, due to compounds like cinnamaldehyde and o-methoxycinnamaldehyde.

Antioxidant: Cinnamon tincture's high antioxidant content helps protect the gums and mouth tissues from damage caused by free radicals, contributing to overall oral health and fresh breath, due to the presence of polyphenols and flavonoids.
Immune support: The immune-boosting properties of cinnamon tincture help the body fight off infections that can lead to bad breath, as cinnamon contains essential oils with immunomodulatory effects.
Astringent: Cinnamon tincture's astringent properties help tighten gum tissues, reducing the risk of gum disease and bad breath, due to tannins found in cinnamon that have astringent properties.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the cinnamon tree is used in medicinal tinctures for bad breath due to its antimicrobial properties that can help kill the bacteria causing bad breath.
Seeds: Cinnamon seeds contain essential oils that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them useful in treating bad breath.
Leaves: Cinnamon leaves have been shown to have antibacterial and antifungal properties, making them effective in reducing bad breath caused by bacteria and fungi.
Buds: The buds of the cinnamon plant contain high levels of eugenol, a compound with antiseptic and analgesic properties, making them useful in treating bad breath caused by infections.
Flowers: Cinnamon flowers contain essential oils that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in treating bad breath caused by inflammation and oxidative stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cinnamomum verum bark, selecting fresh and healthy specimens for optimal quality and potency.
Dry the harvested bark in a warm place or using a food dehydrator on low heat for several hours.
Grind the dried bark into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 1 part powdered cinnamon with 2 parts high-proof vodka or other grain alcohol, storing the mixture in an airtight container for 6-8 weeks.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and using the resulting liquid as desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum tincture helps with bad breath because it contains powerful antibacterial and antimicrobial properties that can effectively eliminate the bacteria and microorganisms responsible for causing bad breath. The main active compound found in Tasmanian blue gum, called eucalyptol, has been shown to have potent antibacterial effects against a variety of oral pathogens, including those that cause halitosis. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of Tasmanian blue gum can help reduce inflammation in the gums and mouth, further preventing the buildup of bacteria and plaque that can lead to bad breath. By using Tasmanian blue gum tincture as a natural and effective mouthwash or gargle, individuals can help freshen their breath and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Tasmanian blue gum tincture's antimicrobial properties help reduce the growth of odor-causing bacteria in the mouth, thanks to the presence of terpinen-4-ol and 1,8-cineole.
Anti-inflammatory: Its anti-inflammatory effects help decrease gum inflammation and irritation, which can contribute to bad breath, due to the presence of α-pinene and limonene.
Antioxidant: The antioxidants found in Tasmanian blue gum tincture, such as gallic acid and rutin, help protect oral tissues from damage and reduce bad breath caused by cellular oxidation.
Expectorant: The expectorant properties of the tincture help clear mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract, reducing the likelihood of bad breath caused by post-nasal drip or lung infections.
Soothing: Tasmanian blue gum tincture has soothing properties that can help alleviate dry mouth symptoms, which can lead to bad breath, due to the presence of linalool and α-terpineol.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antibacterial compounds, the leaves help reduce the growth of odor-causing bacteria in the mouth.
Bark: Containing tannins that have astringent properties, the bark can help control bad breath by tightening and strengthening oral tissues.
Flowers: With natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, the flowers aid in soothing inflamed gums and reducing bad breath.
Seeds: Packed with essential oils, the seeds can help eliminate bad breath by neutralizing foul odors and promoting oral health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality eucalyptus globulus leaves from tasmania and chop them into small pieces.
Combine one part chopped eucalyptus leaves with two parts vodka in a clean glass jar for infusion.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding solids.
Transfer the filtered tincture to small amber bottles and label them with date dosage and preparation instructions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tincture helps with bad breath because it contains potent antibacterial and antimicrobial properties that target the primary cause of bad breath - oral bacteria. These bacteria thrive in the mouth, particularly on the tongue and in between teeth, breaking down food particles and releasing foul-smelling compounds. Fennel's volatile oils, such as anethole, effectively combat these bacteria, reducing their population and, consequently, minimizing the production of unpleasant odors. Moreover, fennel tincture stimulates saliva production, which helps wash away food debris and bacteria, further contributing to fresh breath. Its natural anti-inflammatory properties can also alleviate any potential irritation in the mouth or gums, promoting overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Fennel tincture's antimicrobial properties help reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath, due to the presence of active constituents such as trans-anethole and fenchone.

Carminative: Fennel tincture's carminative properties help prevent the formation of gas in the digestive tract, reducing the likelihood of bad breath caused by digestive issues, due to the presence of active constituents such as estragole and limonene.

Anti-inflammatory: Fennel tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the mouth and digestive tract, alleviating bad breath caused by inflammation, due to the presence of active constituents such as flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Antioxidant: Fennel tincture's antioxidant properties help protect the mouth and digestive tract from oxidative stress, reducing the likelihood of bad breath caused by cellular damage, due to the presence of active constituents such as rosmarinic acid and quercetin.

Expectorant: Fennel tincture's expectorant properties help clear mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract, reducing the likelihood of bad breath caused by post-nasal drip, due to the presence of active constituents such as cineole and alpha-pinene.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are often used in medicinal tinctures for bad breath due to their antimicrobial properties that can help reduce the growth of odor-causing bacteria in the mouth.
Seeds: Fennel seeds have antiseptic and breath-freshening properties, making them a popular choice for treating bad breath in medicinal tinctures.
Leaves: The leaves of the fennel plant have a refreshing aroma and contain essential oils that can help neutralize bad breath and promote oral health.

Flowers: Fennel flowers contain volatile oils that have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful in tinctures for managing bad breath and maintaining a healthy oral environment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems at peak freshness to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator or oven on low heat for 6-8 hours.
Chop the dried foeniculum vulgare into small pieces and combine with 70% vodka in a clean glass jar to create a maceration mixture.
Store the jar in a dark place at room temperature for 2 weeks, shaking daily, to facilitate solvent extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding solids and retaining the resulting herbal tincture for use.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat bad breath with herbal tinctures is by using a combination of potent herbs known for their antibacterial and antimicrobial properties. These herbs include tea tree, sage, peppermint, and clove. A tincture made from these herbs can eliminate the bacteria causing bad breath, thus freshening the breath. Take 1-2 dropperfuls of this herbal tincture, two to three times a day, or as needed, to combat bad breath effectively.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to bad breath that are treated with herbal tinctures include gum diseases and mouth ulcers. Myrrh tincture is often used for its antiseptic properties to treat mouth ulcers, while calendula tincture can be used to soothe inflamed gums. Licorice root tincture is another option, as it can help reduce inflammation and fight off bacteria in the mouth. Additionally, tea tree oil tincture is known for its antifungal and antiviral properties, making it a potential treatment for oral thrush or other fungal infections in the mouth. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal treatments.
